name: DeepCode
tagline: ML-powered code review and bug detection — 'Grammarly for coders'
description: ETH Zurich spin-off that used machine learning to find bugs in code. Acquired by Snyk in 2020, the standalone product was killed and absorbed into Snyk Code.
story: DeepCode was spun off from ETH Zurich in 2016, building a machine learning engine that analyzed code to find bugs and security vulnerabilities — pitched as 'Grammarly for coders.' The company raised $5.2 million from Earlybird and b2venture. DeepCode's AI had been trained on millions of commits from open-source repositories, giving it genuine pattern-matching capability for common coding mistakes. In September 2020, security company Snyk acquired DeepCode to boost its static application security testing capabilities. The DeepCode standalone product was discontinued and the technology was absorbed into Snyk Code. The team survived inside Snyk, but the independent startup and its product were gone — consumed by a larger platform that needed AI bolted onto its existing security offering.
